title: Kafka consumer lag spike on transaction.initiated topic
symptoms: Kafka consumer lag > 50,000 messages on transaction.initiated
Saga start latency increased from 200ms to 45 seconds
Alert fired: KafkaConsumerLagHigh
rootCause: GC pressure caused by a memory leak in payment-orchestrator saga state cache. Full GC pauses lasting 30s caused Kafka poll timeout, triggering consumer rebalance loop.
investigation: Checked Grafana JVM dashboard - heap usage at 98%, long GC pauses
Thread dump showed most threads blocked waiting for GC
Heap dump revealed Saga objects not being evicted from in-memory cache
Cache TTL was not set correctly after refactoring in deploy v2.3.1
resolution: Increased JVM heap to 2GB (-Xmx2g)
Fixed cache TTL configuration in CacheConfig.java
Deployed fix to payment-orchestrator v2.3.2
Manually reset consumer group offset after fix deployed
Backlog cleared within 20 minutes of fix
prevention: Add cache TTL unit test
Alert on heap usage > 85%
Add Kafka consumer lag to SLO dashboard

title: PostgreSQL connection pool exhaustion in ledger-service
symptoms: ledger-service health check failing intermittently
balance.reserve.requested messages queuing in DLQ
Error: Unable to acquire JDBC Connection
All 3 ledger-service pods showing connection timeout errors
rootCause: A long-running database migration (V4__add_index.sql) held a lock on the accounts table for 8 minutes during peak hours. New requests couldn't get connections because all pool connections were waiting for the lock.
investigation: Checked Hikari connection pool metrics - active connections at max (20/20)
pg_stat_activity showed all connections in 'lock wait' state
pg_locks showed lock held by migration flyway process
Migration V4 added index on accounts table without CONCURRENTLY keyword
resolution: Killed the blocking migration process
Redeployed with migration V4 changed to CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY
Increased connection pool to 30 during incident
DLQ messages replayed after service recovery
prevention: All new index migrations must use CONCURRENTLY
Run migrations in separate maintenance window for large tables
Add connection pool saturation alert at 80%
Migration review checklist added to PR template

title: Claude API rate limit causing AI Platform degradation
symptoms: Fraud copilot returning error: 'AI service temporarily unavailable'
Risk engine falling back to rule-only mode
ai-platform error rate > 40% on /ai/fraud/explain endpoint
Anthropic API returning HTTP 429
rootCause: Batch KYC processing job and real-time fraud explanation requests shared the same API key. Batch job consumed all available tokens during the busiest trading hour.
investigation: ai-platform logs showed HTTP 429 from Anthropic API
Usage dashboard showed token consumption spike at 16:00
Traced spike to scheduled batch KYC job that ran at 16:00
Batch job was not rate-limited and consumed 80% of hourly quota
resolution: Stopped batch KYC job
Real-time endpoints recovered as rate limit window reset
Added rate limiter to batch processing (max 10 req/min)
Moved batch KYC to off-peak hours (2 AM - 4 AM)
prevention: Separate API keys for batch vs real-time workloads
Add token usage monitoring and alert at 70% of hourly limit
Implement graceful degradation - return cached/rule-based result when AI unavailable
Queue-based batch processing with rate limiting

title: Outbox scheduler double-publishing events
symptoms: Some transactions appearing to process twice
Duplicate transaction.completed events in Kafka
Balance deducted twice for 23 transactions
Idempotency checks catching some but not all duplicates
rootCause: After transaction-service pod restart during deployment, two pods briefly ran the outbox scheduler simultaneously due to a race condition in leader election. The new pod elected itself before the old pod fully terminated.
investigation: Kafka consumer group showed duplicate message keys
Cross-referenced outbox table - PUBLISHED entries had been re-published
Deployment logs showed rolling update overlap of ~30 seconds
Leader election using Kubernetes lease, but TTL was 60s - too long
resolution: Manually reversed 23 duplicate balance deductions
Reduced lease TTL to 15 seconds
Added distributed lock (Redis SETNX) before outbox batch query
Added unique constraint on Kafka message key + partition in consumer idempotency table
prevention: Add exactly-once semantics to outbox publisher
Idempotency check at consumer level for all saga steps
Deployment strategy changed to Recreate for outbox-scheduler pods
Add monitoring for duplicate event detection

title: Redis connection failure causing rate limiter bypass
symptoms: Redis connection errors in api-gateway logs
Rate limiting not being enforced - high request volumes passing
Backend services receiving 5x normal traffic
Redis sentinel not promoting replica after primary failure
rootCause: Redis primary node OOM-killed at 3:30 AM due to a key expiry scan that allocated excessive memory. Sentinel promotion took 4 minutes due to misconfigured quorum (only 1 of 3 sentinels was responsive).
investigation: Redis MEMORY DOCTOR command showed fragmentation ratio > 5
Large SCAN operations triggered by expired key cleanup
Sentinel logs showed election timeout due to network partition
One sentinel pod was in CrashLoopBackOff state (unrelated issue)
resolution: Manually promoted Redis replica to primary
Fixed crashed sentinel pod
Restarted api-gateway to reconnect to Redis
Rate limiter restored - excessive traffic drained
prevention: Redis memory limit with allkeys-lru eviction policy
Fix all sentinel pods before next incident
api-gateway to fail-closed (allow-list only) when Redis unavailable
Alert on Redis memory usage > 70%

title: Flyway migration failed in production causing rollout block
symptoms: user-service v2.1.0 pods in CrashLoopBackOff
Deployment rollout stuck - 0 of 3 pods ready
Error in logs: Flyway: Migration V3 failed
Old pods (v2.0.5) serving traffic normally
rootCause: V3 migration attempted to add a NOT NULL column without a default value to a table with 500K existing rows. Migration succeeded in staging (empty table) but failed in production.
investigation: Logs showed: column 'push_token_platform' cannot be null
Checked migration V3 - ADD COLUMN push_token_platform VARCHAR NOT NULL
Staging had no users so constraint was fine; production has 500K users
Rollback: old pods were still running (rolling deployment), no data loss
resolution: Fixed migration to add column with DEFAULT ''
Released v2.1.1 with corrected migration
Flyway checksum updated for V3
Production deployment succeeded
prevention: Never add NOT NULL columns without default in migrations
Migration CI test runs against snapshot of production data size
Migration review checklist: check for NOT NULL without DEFAULT
Staging database seeded with representative data volume

title: Notification service WebSocket connections dropped on pod restart
symptoms: ~2,000 WebSocket connections dropped during rolling deployment
Users not receiving real-time transaction status updates
WebSocket reconnection rate spike in client logs
Resolved within 20 minutes as clients reconnected
rootCause: Rolling deployment of notification-service restarted pods that had active WebSocket connections. STOMP clients reconnected automatically but had a 10-second delay.
investigation: Pod restart events correlated with connection drop time
WebSocket sessions are not shared across pods (in-memory registry)
Client-side reconnect logic was implemented but took longer than expected
Termination grace period was only 5 seconds - not enough for graceful WebSocket close
resolution: Increased terminationGracePeriodSeconds to 60
Added SIGTERM handler to send WebSocket close frames before shutdown
Client reconnect timer reduced from 10s to 3s exponential backoff
prevention: Implement sticky sessions for WebSocket connections
Consider distributed WebSocket session registry (Redis-backed)
Client reconnect logic should have < 3s initial retry
Pre-deployment notification via WebSocket before restart

title: Risk engine false positive surge after model update
symptoms: Transaction decline rate jumped from 2% to 18%
Customer support receiving high volume of false decline calls
Risk scores for normal transactions elevated by 20-30 points
REVIEW decisions increased 8x
rootCause: Risk rule configuration update changed velocity check window from 1 hour to 15 minutes without recalibrating thresholds. Lunch hour transaction spike was now hitting the 15-minute limit.
investigation: Grafana risk dashboard showed score distribution shift
Correlated with config-map update deployed at 8:55 AM
Velocity rule: max 5 transactions in window - window changed from 60m to 15m
Average customer makes 3-4 transactions in lunch hour = 3-4 per 15min → triggering
resolution: Reverted velocity check window to 60 minutes
REVIEW transactions auto-cleared via back-office tool
Threshold recalibration process defined before next rule change
prevention: Risk rule changes require A/B testing in canary deployment
Automated alert: decline rate > 5% triggers review
Rule change requires back-test against 30 days of historical transactions
Shadow mode testing for rule changes before production

title: DLQ depth accumulation on balance.rollback.requested
symptoms: Alert: KafkaDlqDepthNonZero for balance.rollback.requested.DLQ
DLQ depth: 847 messages
Some sagas stuck in COMPENSATING state
ledger-service logs: 'Balance lock not found for rollback'
rootCause: Balance lock expiry scheduler ran ahead of schedule and expired locks that were still needed for rollback. Rollback consumer couldn't find the lock and threw exception after 3 retries → DLQ.
investigation: DLQ messages all had same error: BalanceLockNotFoundException
Checked balance_locks table - expired column was true for all affected records
Scheduler ran at 16:30, 15 minutes earlier than expected
Cron expression had timezone issue: running in UTC but configured for IST
resolution: Fixed cron timezone to Asia/Kolkata explicitly
Replayed DLQ messages after regenerating balance locks for affected transactions
Manually resolved stuck sagas via back-office admin endpoint
Verified balance consistency for all affected accounts
prevention: Always specify timezone in cron expressions explicitly
Balance lock rollback should work without lock (use ledger entry to calculate rollback amount)
DLQ replay automation with admin dashboard
Saga stuck detection alert for > 30 min in COMPENSATING state

title: ArgoCD sync failed after Helm values conflict
symptoms: ArgoCD app aegispay-prod showing OutOfSync status
Sync failed: Error: YAML parse error on values-prod.yaml
Production deployment blocked - no new versions deploying
Last good sync was 2 hours ago
rootCause: A CI/CD script used yq to patch image tags but introduced a YAML formatting error (duplicate key in services block). ArgoCD couldn't render Helm templates and rejected the sync.
investigation: ArgoCD UI showed render error on values-prod.yaml
helm lint on the file revealed duplicate key: payment_orchestrator
Git blame showed change was made by the image-tag-update CI job
yq appended instead of updating when key already existed
resolution: Manually fixed YAML in values-prod.yaml and pushed to main
ArgoCD sync succeeded after fix
Updated yq script to use correct merge operator (-i flag with explicit path)
prevention: Add helm lint as step in CI before committing values changes
helm template dry-run in CI pipeline
PR required for values-prod.yaml changes (auto-commit bypasses this)
yamllint pre-commit hook in repository

title: pgvector extension not installed causing ai-platform startup failure
symptoms: ai-platform CrashLoopBackOff on new staging environment
Error: relation 'knowledge_documents' does not exist
Flyway migration V1 failed: extension 'vector' does not exist
New PostgreSQL RDS instance provisioned without pgvector
rootCause: New staging environment was provisioned with standard PostgreSQL RDS without the pgvector extension. The CREATE EXTENSION vector; in V1 migration failed silently and table creation failed.
investigation: Checked RDS parameter groups - pgvector not in installed extensions
Migration V1 had CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector - IF NOT EXISTS masked the error
Table creation after extension install failed because extension wasn't there
Terraform module for RDS didn't include pgvector in engine version requirements
resolution: Enabled pgvector on RDS instance via AWS console
Ran flyway repair to reset migration checksum
Redeployed ai-platform
prevention: Terraform RDS module to enforce pgvector-compatible engine version
Migration V1 should fail FAST if vector extension not installed (remove IF NOT EXISTS)
Environment provisioning checklist includes pgvector verification
Add health check that verifies vector extension present

title: JWT token expiry causing cascade failure across services
symptoms: All authenticated API calls returning 401 at midnight
api-gateway logs: 'JWT expired'
Keycloak logs showing valid token issuance
Mobile apps failing to refresh tokens automatically
rootCause: Service account token used by api-gateway for inter-service calls had a hard-coded expiry of 24 hours, last generated at midnight the previous day. Exact midnight expiry caused all in-flight requests to fail simultaneously.
investigation: Token inspection showed exp claim at midnight exactly
Keycloak service account token generated by startup script at deploy time
Token not being refreshed automatically - only at service startup
Rolling deployment at midnight triggered all pods with same token
resolution: Restarted api-gateway pods to generate fresh tokens
Implemented automatic token refresh 5 minutes before expiry
Changed service account token expiry to 1 hour with auto-refresh
prevention: Never use hard-coded expiry time based on clock time
Implement token refresh before expiry for all service accounts
Add token expiry monitoring alert at 90% of TTL elapsed
Use Kubernetes ServiceAccount tokens with projected volumes (auto-rotated)

title: Saga stuck in RUNNING state after Kafka partition rebalance
symptoms: 15 sagas stuck in RUNNING state for > 30 minutes
balance.commit.requested messages in Kafka but saga not advancing
payment-orchestrator consumer group rebalancing repeatedly
BALANCED consumer lag oscillating up and down
rootCause: payment-orchestrator HPA scaled from 2 to 4 pods during high load, triggering consumer group rebalance. During rebalance, messages were buffered but not processed. Rebalance took unusually long due to all 4 pods joining simultaneously.
investigation: Consumer group status showed REBALANCING for 15 minutes
HPA event log showed scale-up at 13:58
Kafka coordinator logs showed repeated join group requests
Max poll interval (5 min) was being exceeded during rebalance + processing
resolution: Reduced max.poll.records to 10 to speed up poll cycles
Increased session.timeout.ms to account for rebalance time
Stuck sagas advanced manually via admin endpoint after consumer stabilized
SagaTimeoutScheduler automatically caught remaining stuck sagas
prevention: Use static group membership (group.instance.id) to reduce rebalance disruption
Incremental cooperative rebalancing (partition.assignment.strategy=CooperativeStickyAssignor)
Reduce max.poll.records for saga reply consumer
Stuck saga alert threshold reduced from 30m to 15m

title: MongoDB read model out of sync after transaction-service restart
symptoms: Transaction dashboard showing stale status for some transactions
GET /transactions/{id} returning PENDING status for completed transactions
MongoDB transaction_views not reflecting recent state changes
PostgreSQL write model correct; MongoDB read model lagging
rootCause: transaction-service restart caused the CQRS consumer (TransactionReadModelConsumer) to lose its consumer group offset. Kafka auto.offset.reset=latest caused it to skip all events during downtime.
investigation: Compared PostgreSQL and MongoDB for affected transactions - PostgreSQL correct
Consumer group lag for transaction-service-read-model was 0 (wrongly)
Checked consumer group committed offset - reset to latest after pod restart
auto.offset.reset=latest meant events during downtime were skipped
resolution: Changed auto.offset.reset to earliest for read model consumer
Reset consumer group offset to before restart time
Read model consumer replayed and caught up within 15 minutes
Verified consistency for all affected transactions
prevention: Read model consumers should always use auto.offset.reset=earliest
Add consistency check: periodic comparison of PostgreSQL vs MongoDB counts
Admin endpoint to trigger read model rebuild for specific transaction range
Consumer group offset committed more frequently (every 100ms vs 5s)

title: Vault seal after Kubernetes node restart
symptoms: All services returning 'Secret injection failed' errors
ESO operator logs: 'Vault sealed'
All new pods failing to start (can't fetch secrets)
Vault pod running but in sealed state
rootCause: Kubernetes node running Vault pod was rebooted by AWS for maintenance at 3 AM. Vault auto-unsealing was not configured. Vault came back up but sealed, blocking all ESO secret fetches.
investigation: kubectl describe pod vault-0 showed Ready but Vault CLI showed sealed
ESO operator logs confirmed Vault unavailability
Checked Vault auto-unseal config - was not set
AWS KMS key was available and could have been used for auto-unseal
resolution: Manually unsealed Vault using stored unseal keys
ESO operator reconnected automatically within 30 seconds
All pending pods started successfully after Vault became unsealed
prevention: Configure Vault auto-unseal with AWS KMS
Store unseal keys in AWS Secrets Manager for operational recovery
Vault HA setup with 3 nodes to avoid single-node failure
Alert immediately when Vault sealed status detected

title: Stripe webhook signature verification failures
symptoms: Stripe webhook endpoint returning 400 for all events
Stripe dashboard showing webhook delivery failures
payment_intent.succeeded events not advancing sagas
Transactions stuck in PROCESS_PAYMENT step
rootCause: Webhook secret was rotated in Stripe dashboard but the new secret was not updated in Vault. STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET env var had the old value, causing all signature verifications to fail.
investigation: payment-orchestrator logs: SignatureVerificationException
Stripe dashboard showed webhook secret rotation 2 hours prior
Vault secret still had old webhook secret value
ESO refresh interval is 24h - new secret not yet synced
resolution: Updated webhook secret in Vault immediately
Forced ESO ExternalSecret refresh
Rolling restart of payment-orchestrator to pick up new secret
Replayed failed webhook events from Stripe dashboard
prevention: Webhook secret rotation runbook: always update Vault before rotating in Stripe
ESO refresh interval reduced to 1h for payment credentials
Alert on webhook failure rate > 0% in Stripe
Two-phase rotation: new + old secret accepted simultaneously during transition

title: High error rate on /ai/kyc/process endpoint
symptoms: KYC document processing failing for 60% of requests
Error: 'Image quality too low for OCR processing'
Mobile app update released on app stores at 09:00
Error only on iOS devices, not Android
rootCause: iOS app v2.1.0 had a regression in image compression. Documents were being compressed to 15% quality before upload (instead of 75%). OCR model requires minimum 30% quality. Android update (v2.1.1) had the correct compression settings.
investigation: ai-platform logs showed quality score < 0.3 for all failing docs
Downloaded sample document from failing request - very low resolution
Git diff on iOS changes showed compression quality constant changed from 0.75 to 0.15
Android version not affected
resolution: iOS v2.1.1 hotfix released with correct compression quality
Server-side: added quality threshold check before sending to LLM
Users on v2.1.0 asked to update app for KYC functionality
prevention: Add image quality check on server side before OCR processing
Integration test that validates document upload quality end-to-end
iOS image compression constant should be a configuration value, not hardcoded
Canary rollout for mobile apps to detect regressions before full release

title: Notification service email delivery bounce rate spike
symptoms: Gmail SMTP returning hard bounces for 15% of emails
notification-service logs: 'SMTP authentication failed intermittently'
Gmail app password expired after Google policy change
Users not receiving transaction confirmation emails
rootCause: Google changed its security policy to require re-verification of app passwords every 90 days. The app password expired and SMTP authentication started failing intermittently as the grace period expired.
investigation: SMTP error: 535-5.7.8 Username and Password not accepted
Google account security page showed app password expired
Secret in Vault had the 90-day-old password
No alert was configured for email delivery failure rate
resolution: Generated new Gmail app password
Updated Vault secret
Forced ESO refresh and rolling restart of notification-service
Email delivery restored
prevention: Alert on SMTP authentication failure rate > 0%
Calendar reminder for app password rotation every 80 days
Consider migrating to AWS SES for production (no app password expiry)
Email delivery health check endpoint that validates SMTP connection

title: Concurrent saga start caused by duplicate transaction.initiated events
symptoms: 3 transactions had two sagas each - duplicate saga records
Both sagas processed independently - double balance deduction
transaction.initiated topic had duplicate message keys
Outbox scheduler published same entry twice
rootCause: Outbox scheduler had a bug where the same PENDING entry was selected by two threads simultaneously during a database failover. The pessimistic lock on the select query was not being held across the transaction due to connection pool configuration.
investigation: saga table showed two saga rows for same transactionId
outbox_entries table showed same entry marked PUBLISHED twice
Thread dump during failover showed two threads in publishAndMark method
Connection pool returned connections before transaction fully committed
resolution: Fixed: saga.startSaga() now has explicit idempotency check at entry
Fixed outbox scheduler to use SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED
Manually reversed duplicate balance deductions
Retroactively merged duplicate sagas
prevention: SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED for all outbox reads
Saga start must be idempotent at DB level (unique constraint on transactionId)
Add duplicate saga detection alert
Connection pool autocommit must be false for all outbox operations

title: k3s node disk full causing pod evictions
symptoms: Multiple pods being evicted on on-prem k3s node
DiskPressure condition true on node
Postgres container crashed: no space left on device
Logs: kubelet disk eviction threshold exceeded
rootCause: Grafana logs directory grew unbounded over 6 months. /var/log/containers accumulated 45GB of log files. Default k3s log rotation was not configured.
investigation: df -h showed /dev/sda1 at 98% usage
du -sh /var/log/containers showed 45GB
Grafana loki container was logging at DEBUG level generating 500MB/day
No logrotate configured for container logs
resolution: Deleted old log files to free space
Configured logrotate for container logs
Changed Grafana log level to WARN
Added 500GB additional disk and mounted at /var/lib/rancher
prevention: Monitor node disk usage > 70% alert
Configure container log rotation (max-size: 10m, max-file: 3) in k3s config
Set log levels to WARN or higher for infrastructure components
Scheduled disk cleanup cron for old logs

title: TLS certificate expiry causing HTTPS failures on production ingress
symptoms: All HTTPS requests to aegispay.shreyasshelar.uk returning SSL certificate error
Mobile app showing 'Connection not secure' error to all users
cert-manager logs: CertificateRequest expired before renewal
Let's Encrypt ACME challenge failing for domain validation
rootCause: cert-manager's ACME HTTP-01 challenge was blocked because a new WAF rule deployed 3 days earlier was stripping the .well-known/acme-challenge path. Certificate expired at midnight before auto-renewal completed.
investigation: kubectl describe certificate aegispay-tls showed Not Ready and Last Failure: ACME challenge failed
Cloudflare WAF logs showed 403 responses for /.well-known/acme-challenge/*
WAF rule added 3 days ago had a catch-all block rule for dot-files
cert-manager had been silently failing renewals for 3 days
resolution: Added WAF exception for /.well-known/acme-challenge/* path
Triggered manual certificate renewal: kubectl annotate certificate aegispay-tls cert-manager.io/issue-once=true
Certificate renewed within 2 minutes after WAF exception applied
HTTPS restored for all users
prevention: Alert on certificate expiry < 30 days
Test ACME challenge path in CI after any WAF/Ingress rule change
Consider using DNS-01 challenge instead of HTTP-01 to avoid WAF dependency
cert-manager renewal failure should trigger alert, not just log

title: Stripe webhook replay storm after Vault recovery
symptoms: payment-orchestrator processing 500+ sagas/minute (10x normal)
Duplicate COMPLETED and FAILED events for same transactions
Stripe dashboard showing 3,000+ webhook replays queued
DLQ depth rising on balance.commit.requested topic
rootCause: Vault sealed at 3 AM (node restart), payment-orchestrator pods couldn't start for 1 hour. During downtime, Stripe's webhook retry mechanism queued all failed delivery attempts. When Vault was unsealed and pods came back up at 4 AM, Stripe replayed all 3,000 queued webhooks simultaneously. Idempotency check only covered in-flight requests, not persisted saga state.
investigation: Stripe webhook events dashboard showed burst of 3,000 deliveries at 04:01
payment-orchestrator saga table had duplicate sagas for 47 transactions
Idempotency table checked by SagaOrchestrator.startSaga() but not by onPaymentProcessed()
Saga state transitions lacked unique constraint - allowed duplicate COMPLETED events
resolution: Added unique constraint on saga_id + status in saga_events table
Added idempotency check at every saga step transition, not just start
Rolled back 47 duplicate balance operations manually
Stripe webhook replay is now idempotent end-to-end
prevention: All saga event transitions must be idempotent at DB level
Webhook idempotency: use Stripe event ID as idempotency key, store in processed_events table
Slow down Stripe webhook consumption rate on startup using a token bucket
Vault auto-unseal via AWS KMS (prevents 1-hour downtime window)

title: HPA scale-down during peak causing DLQ accumulation
symptoms: Alert: KafkaDlqDepthNonZero for risk.assessment.requested.DLQ
DLQ depth: 1,200 messages over 1 hour
risk-engine processing latency jumped from 80ms to 8 seconds
HPA scaled risk-engine from 4 pods to 2 pods at 18:25
rootCause: HPA scale-down from 4 to 2 pods coincided with late-evening transaction spike (payday). Two risk-engine pods were terminated while they had active Kafka message processing in flight. preStop hook was not configured — SIGTERM immediately killed threads mid-processing, sending uncommitted messages to DLQ after retry exhaustion.
investigation: HPA event log showed scale-down at 18:25, DLQ accumulation started at 18:27
Consumer group showed two pods leaving abruptly (no graceful close)
DLQ messages had header dlq-failure-reason: Consumer was shut down
HPA stabilization window was 0 seconds - allowed immediate aggressive scale-down
resolution: Added preStop: exec: command: ['sleep', '30'] to risk-engine pods
Increased terminationGracePeriodSeconds to 60 for risk-engine
Set HPA scaleDown stabilizationWindowSeconds to 300 to prevent scale-down during traffic spikes
Replayed 1,200 DLQ messages after stabilization via admin endpoint
prevention: All Kafka consumer pods must have preStop sleep hook
HPA scaleDown.stabilizationWindowSeconds >= 300 for Kafka consumers
Alert when DLQ depth > 0 within 5 minutes of HPA scale-down event
Consider max.poll.records reduction to ensure poll interval fits within grace period

title: Split-brain in Redis Sentinel causing double rate-limit bypass
symptoms: Rate limiter counters inconsistent across api-gateway pods
Some users able to make 200+ requests per minute (limit: 100)
Redis Sentinel showing two primaries simultaneously
api-gateway logs: Jedis connection switching between two primaries
rootCause: Network partition between sentinel-0 and sentinel-1/sentinel-2. sentinel-0 (connected to original primary) could not reach the other sentinels and did not trigger failover. sentinel-1 and sentinel-2 quorum-elected the replica as new primary. Both primaries accepted writes for 8 minutes. Different api-gateway pods connected to different primaries, getting divergent rate limit counters.
investigation: Redis SENTINEL masters showed two different IPs reported as primary
Network trace showed packet loss between sentinel-0 and the rest of the cluster
Lettuce client reconnected to new primary after sentinel-1/2 announcement
Old primary continued serving the 2 api-gateway pods still connected to it
resolution: Restored network connectivity - automatic sentinel election resolved split-brain
Forced SLAVEOF on original primary to become replica of new primary
Data loss: 8 minutes of rate limit counter increments lost (acceptable - safety conservatively resets counters)
api-gateway reconnected to correct primary
prevention: Redis Sentinel quorum should be ceil(n/2)+1 for strict majority
Monitor for multiple primaries: alert if SENTINEL masters count > 1
Consider Redis Cluster mode for production (better partition tolerance than Sentinel)
api-gateway rate limiter fail-closed on Redis unavailability (deny requests, not allow)
